skill.name: zaker-news-search
skill.description: Search ZAKER news by keyword and optional dates within the supported 30-day window.
skill.summary: Find recent ZAKER news articles for topics, people, companies, and events.
skill.author: ZAKER-coder

security_audit.summary: The static network and shell findings are mostly expected for a news search skill that calls the ZAKER article API. No malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration beyond user search terms was found, but publication should disclose outbound requests and bundled executable examples.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Requests to ZAKER API
medium_findings.description: The skill sends search keywords and optional date parameters to https://skills.myzaker.com. This is core functionality, but users should know their queries leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and request calls are explicit in code and documentation. The behavior appears intentional for news retrieval, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Executable Shell Example Performs Network Calls
medium_findings.description: The shell script invokes curl and pipes responses to jq. The commands use fixed sample queries, so command injection was not found, but the script executes external tools and contacts a third-party API.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl invocations are directly present and executable. Risk is moderated because the arguments are hardcoded examples, not untrusted shell interpolation.

low_findings.title: Python Request Has No Explicit Timeout
low_findings.description: The Python example catches request exceptions but does not set a timeout. This can cause a long hang if the API is slow or unreachable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The requests.get call is visible and has no timeout argument. This is an availability concern rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline Markdown backticks around parameter names, examples, and response fields. These are documentation formatting markers, not runtime shell or Ruby execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and table entries. No interpreter evaluates these backticks during skill use.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The cited front matter description does not contain a cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation. No evidence found of weak hashing or encryption behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a natural-language skill description. It contains no crypto code, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive processing.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md file is multilingual Markdown with Chinese and English text. No evidence found of binary payloads, encrypted content, obfuscation, or hidden instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows readable Markdown front matter and prose. The entropy heuristic is likely affected by mixed-language text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Third-Party API Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses a fixed ZAKER API URL for article search. This is necessary for the skill, but it creates a dependency on an external service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The same API endpoint is consistently documented and used in examples. It is a real outbound dependency, not an obfuscated destination.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Invokes curl
dangerous_patterns.description: The included shell script uses curl to query the ZAKER endpoint and jq to format output. No user-controlled command injection path was found.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The curl commands are explicit and easy to verify. Their arguments are static examples, which limits injection risk.

content.value_statement: Recent news research can be noisy and difficult to verify. This skill searches ZAKER articles by keyword and optional date range.
actual_capabilities: Supports optional start and end time parameters.
Returns article titles, authors, publish times, and summaries.
Uses a documented unauthenticated GET endpoint.
Provides Python and shell examples for API access.
limitations: Date filtering is limited to the most recent 30 days.
Results depend on ZAKER API availability and coverage.
The Chinese news corpus is stronger than English coverage.
Each request returns a limited result set.

use_cases.title: Track a breaking topic
use_cases.description: Find recent ZAKER articles about an event, company, person, or policy topic.

use_cases.title: Check a time-limited story
use_cases.description: Search for reports within a supported date window to understand recent developments.

use_cases.title: Expand a related-news workflow
use_cases.description: Use keyword searches after seeing a headline to find more context from ZAKER sources.

prompt_templates.title: Basic keyword search
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for recent news on one topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ZAKER news for articles about artificial intelligence.

prompt_templates.title: Company news search
prompt_templates.scenario: Find recent reports about a company or product.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ZAKER for recent news about Tesla and summarize the main themes.

prompt_templates.title: Date filtered search
prompt_templates.scenario: Limit results to a supported recent date range.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ZAKER news for stock market reports from 2026-06-20 00:00:00 to 2026-06-27 23:59:59.

prompt_templates.title: Compare related topics
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ZAKER for AI regulation and autonomous driving policy. Compare the article themes and source coverage.

output_examples.input: Search ZAKER news for artificial intelligence.
output_examples.output: A concise list of recent articles with title, author, publish time, and short summary for each result.

output_examples.input: Find ZAKER reports about iPhone 15 after 2024-01-01.
output_examples.output: A date-filtered news list focused on matching iPhone 15 articles, with source details and summaries.

best_practices: Use concise keywords for better matching.
Provide exact dates when you need a time range.
Review article summaries before drawing conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not send private or sensitive keywords without consent.
Do not expect results outside the supported 30-day date window.
Do not treat search results as complete coverage of every source.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The documented ZAKER endpoint is used without authentication.

faq.question: What information is sent to ZAKER?
faq.answer: The search keyword and optional date parameters are sent in the request.

faq.question: Can it search old news?
faq.answer: The documented date filter supports only the most recent 30 days.

faq.question: Does it work better in Chinese or English?
faq.answer: It supports both, but the documentation states Chinese coverage is more complete.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning that searches make outbound requests to the ZAKER API.
